data.content:
### Generate Plugin Settings Markdown File

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/examples/create-settings-command.md

Template for creating `.claude/my-plugin.local.md` configuration file with frontmatter containing validation settings and user-defined preferences. File includes enabled status, validation mode, file size limits, and error notification settings.

```markdown
---
enabled: <true if Yes, false if No>
validation_mode: <strict, standard, or lenient>
max_file_size: 1000000
notify_on_errors: true
---

# Plugin Configuration

Your plugin is configured with <mode> validation mode.

To modify settings, edit this file and restart Claude Code.
```

--------------------------------

### Document Claude Code Restart Requirement for Settings Changes in Markdown

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

This markdown snippet provides clear instructions for users on how to apply changes made to `.claude/my-plugin.local.md` settings. It emphasizes the necessity of saving, exiting, and restarting Claude Code for new settings to be loaded, due to hooks not being hot-swapped during a session.

```markdown
## Changing Settings

After editing `.claude/my-plugin.local.md`:
1. Save the file
2. Exit Claude Code
3. Restart: `claude` or `cc`
4. New settings will be loaded
```

--------------------------------

### Outline Steps for Creating Settings Files via Commands in Markdown

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

This markdown snippet outlines a procedure for a command to create a `.claude/my-plugin.local.md` settings file. It details steps from asking user preferences to setting values in YAML frontmatter and informing the user about the need to restart Claude Code for changes to take effect.

```markdown
# Setup Command

Steps:
1. Ask user for configuration preferences
2. Create `.claude/my-plugin.local.md` with YAML frontmatter
3. Set appropriate values based on user input
4. Inform user that settings are saved
5. Remind user to restart Claude Code for hooks to recognize changes
```

--------------------------------

### Configured Agent with Plugin Settings Adaptation

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

Markdown pattern for an agent that checks for plugin settings at `.claude/my-plugin.local.md` and adapts its behavior based on configuration fields like enabled status and processing mode.

```markdown
---
name: configured-agent
description: Agent that adapts to project settings
---

Check for plugin settings at `.claude/my-plugin.local.md`.
If present, parse YAML frontmatter and adapt behavior according to:
- enabled: Whether plugin is active
- mode: Processing mode (strict, standard, lenient)
- Additional configuration fields
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Plugin Behavior with YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

This YAML frontmatter defines configuration settings for a plugin, including `validation_level`, `max_file_size`, `allowed_extensions`, and `enable_logging`. These settings enable configuration-driven behavior, allowing the plugin to adjust its operations based on project-specific requirements without code changes.

```markdown
---validation_level: strict
max_file_size: 1000000
allowed_extensions: [".js", ".ts", ".tsx"]
enable_logging: true
---

# Validation Configuration

Strict mode enabled for this project.
All writes validated against security policies.
```

--------------------------------

### Lazy Load Settings Only When Needed in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/references/parsing-techniques.md

Implements lazy loading pattern that performs quick checks before accessing the settings file. Exits early if conditions don't require settings parsing, reducing unnecessary file I/O. Useful for scripts that may not always need configuration data.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)

# Quick checks first (no file I/O)
tool_name=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_name')
if [[ "$tool_name" != "Write" ]]; then
  exit 0  # Not a write operation, skip
fi

# Only now check settings file
if [[ -f ".claude/my-plugin.local.md" ]]; then
  # Parse settings
  # ...
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Plugin Settings File Template with YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

Template showing the standard structure for plugin configuration files with YAML frontmatter and markdown body. The frontmatter contains configuration fields (enabled, settings, lists) and markdown body provides additional context or instructions.

```markdown
---
enabled: true
setting1: value1
setting2: value2
numeric_setting: 42
list_setting: ["item1", "item2"]
---

# Additional Context

This markdown body can contain:
- Task descriptions
- Additional instructions
- Prompts to feed back to Claude
- Documentation or notes
```

--------------------------------

### Create Multi-Agent Swarm Plugin Settings File with Bash Here-Document

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/references/real-world-examples.md

This Bash command snippet shows how to create the `.claude/multi-agent-swarm.local.md` settings file using a here-document. It populates the YAML frontmatter fields and the Markdown body with dynamic variables like `AGENT_NAME`, `TASK_ID`, `COORDINATOR_SESSION`, `TASK_DESCRIPTION`, and `TASK_DETAILS` during the swarm launch process.

```bash
cat > "$WORKTREE_PATH/.claude/multi-agent-swarm.local.md" <<EOF
---
agent_name: $AGENT_NAME
task_number: $TASK_ID
pr_number: TBD
coordinator_session: $COORDINATOR_SESSION
enabled: true
dependencies: [$DEPENDENCIES]
additional_instructions: "$EXTRA_INSTRUCTIONS"---

# Task: $TASK_DESCRIPTION

$TASK_DETAILS
EOF
```

--------------------------------

### Process Command with Plugin Settings Configuration

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

Markdown documentation pattern for a command that reads plugin settings from `.claude/my-plugin.local.md`, parses the YAML frontmatter, and applies configuration to customize command behavior.

```markdown
---
description: Process data with plugin
allowed-tools: ["Read", "Bash"]
---

# Process Command

Steps:
1. Check if settings exist at `.claude/my-plugin.local.md`
2. Read configuration using Read tool
3. Parse YAML frontmatter to extract settings
4. Apply settings to processing logic
5. Execute with configured behavior
```

--------------------------------

### Set Environment Variables for User Configuration (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/mcp-integration/references/authentication.md

Provides bash commands to set `WORKSPACE_ID` and `TENANT_ID` environment variables. These variables can then be used by applications or plugins to configure per-user or per-workspace settings, enabling dynamic behavior based on the user's context.

```bash
export WORKSPACE_ID="my-workspace-123"
export TENANT_ID="my-company"
```

--------------------------------

### Extract Markdown Body from Settings File in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

Bash technique to extract the markdown content that appears after the closing --- marker in a plugin settings file using awk.

```bash
# Get everything after closing ---
BODY=$(awk '/^---$/{i++; next} i>=2' "$FILE")
```

--------------------------------

### Markdown File Structure with YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/references/parsing-techniques.md

Illustrates the expected structure of a settings file, including YAML frontmatter and a markdown body. This is a reference for the parsing examples that follow.

```markdown
---
field1: value1
field2: "value with spaces"
numeric_field: 42
boolean_field: true
list_field: ["item1", "item2", "item3"]
---

# Markdown Content

This body content can be extracted separately.
It's useful for prompts, documentation, or additional context.
```

--------------------------------

### Debug Bash Script with Tracing and Parsed Values

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/references/parsing-techniques.md

Enables debug tracing and outputs parsed configuration values for troubleshooting. Uses set -x to enable command tracing and redirects debug output to stderr. Helps identify parsing issues and verify that values are extracted correctly.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -x  # Enable debug tracing

FILE=".claude/my-plugin.local.md"

if [[ -f "$FILE" ]]; then
  echo "Settings file found" >&2

  FRONTMATTER=$(sed -n '/^---$/,/^---$/{ /^---$/d; p; }' "$FILE")
  echo "Frontmatter:" >&2
  echo "$FRONTMATTER" >&2

  ENABLED=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep '^enabled:' | sed 's/enabled: */''/)
  echo "Enabled: $ENABLED" >&2
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Define Multi-Agent Swarm Plugin Settings with Markdown Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/references/real-world-examples.md

This Markdown file with YAML frontmatter defines the configuration and task details for a 'multi-agent-swarm' plugin. It includes metadata like agent name, task number, PR number, coordinator session, and an enabled flag, along with the main task description and requirements in the Markdown body. This structure allows plugins to store and retrieve structured settings and task instructions.

```markdown
---agent_name: auth-implementation
task_number: 3.5
pr_number: 1234
coordinator_session: team-leader
enabled: true
dependencies: ["Task 3.4"]
additional_instructions: "Use JWT tokens, not sessions"---# Task: Implement Authentication

Build JWT-based authentication for the REST API.

## Requirements
- JWT token generation and validation
- Refresh token flow
- Secure password hashing

## Success Criteria
- Auth endpoints implemented
- Tests passing (100% coverage)
- PR created and CI green
- Documentation updated

## Coordination
Depends on Task 3.4 (user model).
Report status to 'team-leader' session.
```

--------------------------------

### Advanced Plugin Configuration with Multiple Settings

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/examples/example-settings.md

Implements comprehensive plugin configuration including file size limits, allowed extensions, logging levels, retry logic, and custom paths. Supports granular control over plugin behavior with validation modes, timeouts, and extension filtering for security and performance optimization.

```markdown
---
enabled: true
strict_mode: false
max_file_size: 1000000
allowed_extensions: [".js", ".ts", ".tsx"]
enable_logging: true
notification_level: info
retry_attempts: 3
timeout_seconds: 60
custom_path: "/path/to/data"
---

# My Plugin Advanced Configuration

This project uses custom plugin configuration with:
- Standard validation mode
- 1MB file size limit
- JavaScript/TypeScript files allowed
- Info-level logging
- 3 retry attempts

## Additional Notes

Contact @team-lead with questions about this configuration.
```

--------------------------------

### Sanitize User Input Before Writing to Settings Files in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

This Bash script demonstrates how to escape double quotes in user input before writing it into a settings file with YAML frontmatter. This sanitization step prevents injection issues and ensures that user-provided strings are correctly interpreted within the YAML structure, enhancing security.

```bash
# Escape quotes in user input
SAFE_VALUE=$(echo "$USER_INPUT" | sed 's/"/\\"/g')

# Write to file
cat > "$STATE_FILE" <<EOF
---
user_setting: "$SAFE_VALUE"
---
EOF
```

--------------------------------

### Implement Default Settings for Claude Code Hooks in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

This Bash script demonstrates how to provide sensible default values for variables (`ENABLED`, `MODE`) if a settings file (`$STATE_FILE`) does not exist. This ensures the script or plugin can operate even without explicit user configuration, falling back to predefined defaults.

```bash
if [[ ! -f "$STATE_FILE" ]]; then
  # Use defaults
  ENABLED=true
  MODE=standard
else
  # Read from file
  # ...
fi
```

--------------------------------

### AskUserQuestion JSON Structure for Plugin Configuration

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/examples/create-settings-command.md

JSON configuration for gathering user preferences about plugin enablement and validation mode. Uses multi-select set to false for single-choice questions with descriptive options.

```json
{
  "questions": [
    {
      "question": "Enable plugin for this project?",
      "header": "Enable Plugin",
      "multiSelect": false,
      "options": [
        {
          "label": "Yes",
          "description": "Plugin will be active"
        },
        {
          "label": "No",
          "description": "Plugin will be disabled"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "question": "Validation mode?",
      "header": "Mode",
      "multiSelect": false,
      "options": [
        {
          "label": "Strict",
          "description": "Maximum validation and security checks"
        },
        {
          "label": "Standard",
          "description": "Balanced validation (recommended)"
        },
        {
          "label": "Lenient",
          "description": "Minimal validation only"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Provide Configuration Template for Claude Code Plugin in Markdown

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

This markdown snippet provides a template for users to create a `.claude/my-plugin.local.md` file in their projects. It includes example YAML frontmatter with `enabled`, `mode`, and `max_retries` settings, and advises users to restart Claude Code after making changes for the settings to become active.

```markdown
## Configuration

Create `.claude/my-plugin.local.md` in your project:

```markdown
---
enabled: true
mode: standard
max_retries: 3
---

# Plugin Configuration

Your settings are active.
```

After creating or editing, restart Claude Code for changes to take effect.
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Git to Ignore Claude Code Local Settings Files

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

This `.gitignore` entry specifies patterns to exclude local Claude Code settings files from version control. By ignoring `.claude/*.local.md` and `.claude/*.local.json`, it ensures that user-specific or environment-specific configurations are not committed to the repository, maintaining clean and portable projects.

```gitignore
.claude/*.local.md
.claude/*.local.json
```

--------------------------------

### Extract Frontmatter Section from Settings File in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

Bash technique to extract the complete YAML frontmatter section (content between --- markers) from a plugin settings file using sed.

```bash
# Extract everything between --- markers
FRONTMATTER=$(sed -n '/^---$/,/^---$/{ /^---$/d; p; }' "$FILE")
```

--------------------------------

### Temporarily Active Hooks Pattern in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

Complete bash pattern for implementing hooks that can be temporarily enabled/disabled via a plugin settings file. Checks for settings file existence, reads enabled flag, and skips execution if disabled.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
STATE_FILE=".claude/security-scan.local.md"

# Quick exit if not configured
if [[ ! -f "$STATE_FILE" ]]; then
  exit 0
fi

# Read enabled flag
FRONTMATTER=$(sed -n '/^---$/,/^---$/{ /^---$/d; p; }' "$STATE_FILE")
ENABLED=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep '^enabled:' | sed 's/enabled: */')

if [[ "$ENABLED" != "true" ]]; then
  exit 0  # Disabled
fi

# Run hook logic
# ...
```

--------------------------------

### Parse YAML Frontmatter from Settings File in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

Bash script demonstrating how to read and parse YAML frontmatter from a plugin settings file. Extracts individual boolean and string fields from the frontmatter section between --- markers using sed and grep.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Define state file path
STATE_FILE=".claude/my-plugin.local.md"

# Quick exit if file doesn't exist
if [[ ! -f "$STATE_FILE" ]]; then
  exit 0  # Plugin not configured, skip
fi

# Parse YAML frontmatter (between --- markers)
FRONTMATTER=$(sed -n '/^---$/,/^---$/{ /^---$/d; p; }' "$STATE_FILE")

# Extract individual fields
ENABLED=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep '^enabled:' | sed 's/enabled: *//' | sed 's/^"\(.*\)"$/\1/')
STRICT_MODE=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep '^strict_mode:' | sed 's/strict_mode: *//' | sed 's/^"\(.*\)"$/\1/')

# Check if enabled
if [[ "$ENABLED" != "true" ]]; then
  exit 0  # Disabled
fi

# Use configuration in hook logic
if [[ "$STRICT_MODE" == "true" ]]; then
  # Apply strict validation
  # ...
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Parse Multiple YAML Fields with Caching in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/references/parsing-techniques.md

Parses YAML frontmatter once and extracts multiple fields from cached result instead of re-parsing the file. Improves performance by reducing file I/O operations when accessing multiple configuration values. Demonstrates best practice for reading settings multiple times.

```bash
# Parse once
FRONTMATTER=$(sed -n '/^---$/,/^---$/{ /^---$/d; p; }' "$FILE")

# Extract multiple fields from cached frontmatter
FIELD1=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep '^field1:' | sed 's/field1: */''/)
FIELD2=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep '^field2:' | sed 's/field2: */''/)
FIELD3=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep '^field3:' | sed 's/field3: */''/)
```

--------------------------------

### Define Ralph-Wiggum Plugin Loop Settings with Markdown Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/references/real-world-examples.md

This Markdown file, `.claude/ralph-loop.local.md`, defines the state and configuration for a 'ralph-wiggum' looping plugin. It includes frontmatter fields like `iteration`, `max_iterations`, `completion_promise`, and `started_at`, along with task instructions in the Markdown body. This structure allows the plugin to manage its iterative execution state and loop control.

```markdown
---iteration: 1
max_iterations: 10
completion_promise: "All tests passing and build successful"
started_at: "2025-01-15T14:30:00Z"---

Fix all the linting errors in the project.
Make sure tests pass after each fix.
Document any changes needed in CLAUDE.md.
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Numeric Range for Settings in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

This Bash script validates a `max_value` extracted from `FRONTMATTER`, ensuring it is a number within a specified range (1-100). If the value is invalid, it outputs a warning and assigns a default value, preventing erroneous behavior from malformed configuration.

```bash
MAX=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep '^max_value:' | sed 's/max_value: *//')

# Validate numeric range
if ! [[ "$MAX" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || [[ $MAX -lt 1 ]] || [[ $MAX -gt 100 ]]; then
  echo "⚠️  Invalid max_value in settings (must be 1-100)" >&2
  MAX=10  # Use default
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Parse Plugin Configuration in Bash Hooks

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/examples/example-settings.md

Extracts YAML frontmatter from markdown configuration files and reads settings for conditional hook execution. Uses sed to isolate frontmatter section and grep to parse individual configuration keys, enabling dynamic hook behavior based on plugin settings.

```bash
# Check if plugin is configured
if [[ ! -f ".claude/my-plugin.local.md" ]]; then
  exit 0  # Not configured, skip hook
fi

# Read settings
FRONTMATTER=$(sed -n '/^---$/,/^---$/{ /^---$/d; p; }' ".claude/my-plugin.local.md")
ENABLED=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep '^enabled:' | sed 's/enabled: *//'')

# Apply settings
if [[ "$ENABLED" == "true" ]]; then
  # Hook is active
  # ...
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Update Multi-Agent Swarm Plugin PR Number in Settings File with Sed

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/references/real-world-examples.md

This Bash command demonstrates how to update a specific field, `pr_number`, within the `.claude/multi-agent-swarm.local.md` settings file. It uses `sed` to find and replace the `pr_number` line with a new value, creating a temporary file and then atomically replacing the original file. This is a common pattern for modifying configuration files in shell scripts.

```bash
sed "s/^pr_number: .*/pr_number: $PR_NUM/" \
  ".claude/multi-agent-swarm.local.md" > temp.md
mv temp.md ".claude/multi-agent-swarm.local.md"
```

--------------------------------

### Plugin Configuration File Directory Structure

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

Defines the standard directory structure for storing plugin configuration files within a Claude Code project. Configuration files use the .local.md extension for local, non-versioned settings.

```text
project-root/
└── .claude/
    └── plugin-name.local.md
```

--------------------------------

### Plugin State File Example with YAML Configuration

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

Concrete example of a plugin state file (`.claude/my-plugin.local.md`) demonstrating typical configuration fields like enabled flag, mode settings, retry limits, notification levels, and session references with markdown documentation.

```markdown
---
enabled: true
strict_mode: false
max_retries: 3
notification_level: info
coordinator_session: team-leader
---

# Plugin Configuration

This plugin is configured for standard validation mode.
Contact @team-lead with questions.
```

--------------------------------

### Implement Atomic File Updates in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/references/parsing-techniques.md

Outlines the best practice for updating settings files atomically. This involves writing changes to a temporary file and then performing an atomic `mv` operation, which prevents file corruption during updates.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
FILE=".claude/my-plugin.local.md"
NEW_VALUE="updated_value"

# Create temp file
TEMP_FILE="${FILE}.tmp.$$"

# Update field using sed
sed "s/^field_name: .*/field_name: $NEW_VALUE/" "$FILE" > "$TEMP_FILE"

# Atomic replace
mv "$TEMP_FILE" "$FILE"
```

--------------------------------

### Gitignore Pattern for Local Plugin Settings

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/examples/example-settings.md

Prevents committing user-specific plugin configuration files to version control. Uses wildcard patterns to exclude all .local.md and .local.json files from the .claude directory, ensuring configuration remains local and not shared across team members.

```gitignore
# Plugin settings (user-local, not committed)
.claude/*.local.md
.claude/*.local.json
```

--------------------------------

### Set User Environment Variables for stdio Server Credentials

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/mcp-integration/references/authentication.md

This Bash snippet illustrates how a user would set environment variables in their shell to provide credentials for a stdio server. These variables, such as `DATABASE_URL`, `DB_USER`, and `DB_PASSWORD`, are then picked up by the Claude Code plugin's configuration for authentication.

```bash
# User sets these in their shell
export DATABASE_URL="postgresql://localhost/mydb"
export DB_USER="myuser"
export DB_PASSWORD="mypassword"
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Plugin Configuration in Markdown Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/examples/example-settings.md

Creates a basic plugin configuration file using markdown with YAML frontmatter. This establishes the minimum required settings (enabled status and mode) for a plugin to function. The file should be named with .local.md suffix to prevent version control commits.

```markdown
---
enabled: true
mode: standard
---

# My Plugin Configuration

Plugin is active in standard mode.
```

--------------------------------

### Extract YAML Frontmatter Block in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/references/parsing-techniques.md

Demonstrates how to use `sed` to extract the entire YAML frontmatter block from a settings file. It captures all lines between the opening and closing `---` markers, excluding the markers themselves.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
FILE=".claude/my-plugin.local.md"

# Extract everything between --- markers (excluding the markers themselves)
FRONTMATTER=$(sed -n '/^---$/,/^---$/{ /^---$/d; p; }' "$FILE")
```

--------------------------------

### Handle Empty and Null Field Values in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/references/parsing-techniques.md

Safely extracts field values and provides default fallback for empty or null entries. Checks for both empty strings and literal 'null' values. Useful for optional configuration fields that may be missing or explicitly set to null.

```bash
VALUE=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep '^field1:' | sed 's/field1: */''/)
# VALUE will be empty string

# Check for empty/null
if [[ -z "$VALUE" ]] || [[ "$VALUE" == "null" ]]; then
  VALUE="default"
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Edit Plugin Settings and Restart Claude Code

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/examples/example-settings.md

Workflow for manually editing plugin configuration files and applying changes through restart. Requires explicit Claude Code process termination and restart to reload configuration since hooks cannot be hot-swapped during runtime.

```bash
# Edit settings
vim .claude/my-plugin.local.md

# Changes take effect after restart
exit  # Exit Claude Code
claude  # Restart
```
